model.label: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
model.description: Google • Preview Gemini model tuned for high-end multimodal reasoning and long-context analysis.

pick.rationale: Kentucky enters this 7/10 matchup with a slight statistical edge, ranking 28th in KenPom compared to Santa Clara's 35th. The game presents a classic clash of styles, pitting the Broncos' elite top-25 offense against the Wildcats' stout top-35 defense. While Santa Clara has had an impressive 26-win season in the WCC—including a run to their conference tournament title game—their 76th-ranked defense may struggle to contain Kentucky's balanced attack. The Wildcats' battle-tested resume in the SEC and superior overall efficiency margin give them the necessary edge to survive a potential upset bid and advance.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze Kentucky vs Santa Clara Matchup
reasoningStep.summary: Kentucky has a slight edge in overall efficiency and a more balanced profile, while Santa Clara relies heavily on an elite offense.
evidence: Kentucky ranks 28th in KenPom and 32nd in Torvik, with a top-35 defense and top-40 offense.
Santa Clara ranks 35th in KenPom and 28th in Torvik, boasting a top-25 offense but a vulnerable 76th-ranked defense.
Santa Clara had a strong 26-win season in the WCC, but Kentucky's SEC schedule and superior overall efficiency margin make them the favorite.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
